text:
Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Melody. She lived on a quiet street where every day she would play with her imaginary friends: Polly the parrot, Gary the golden retriever, and Bella the badger. Their adventures were never ordinary; they'd soar on clouds, swim through pools of stars, and chase rainbows. One windy autumn afternoon, after gathering acorns for a treasure hunt she imagined in their grand oak tree, Melody discovered an old, weathered box buried beneath some gnarled limbs.

Inside, nestled amidst fallen leaves, were colorful pebbles. Intrigued, Melody picked them up. One sparkled intensely. It felt different. She rubbed it—just a single stone. Suddenly, the stars above twinkled with light. The winds whispered secrets, songs of distant lands.

She wondered if any adventure dwelt inside these relics. Gathering her courage, she chipped off a shard. Presto! It glowed warmer.
